behaviorsAudited: createBridgeTool('FileRead') routes to FileReadView when content carries the <read-content> envelope; createBridgeTool('Read') (the legacy wrong pre-seed name) does NOT route — exact-case + correct-name dispatch
FileReadView renders the file path bolded, optionally with a [startLine-endLine] range, followed by the file content body
If the content is empty, FileReadView renders an (empty file) indicator instead of an empty Box
message-adapter.formatStructuredToolResult for tool_call_completed with toolName='FileRead' produces the <read-file>/<read-lines>/<read-content> envelope shape FileReadView consumes
Pre-seeded createBridgeTools list now contains 'FileRead' (canonical live name), removing the previously wrong 'Read' pre-seed

edgeCasesAudited: FileRead result with content longer than 8KB is truncated to ~8KB with a [N chars truncated] marker (no megabyte-scale renders)
FileRead result missing the path field (slice-only result) renders the body without a header instead of throwing
FileRead result missing the content field (zero-length read) renders the (empty file) indicator
FileRead with the legacy single-string content shape (no envelope) falls through to the generic Text renderer without crashing
FileRead with non-string startLine/endLine falls back to omitting the line range header instead of emitting <read-lines> with non-numeric values
createBridgeTool('Read') (the wrong pre-seed name) does NOT route to FileReadView even with the correct envelope content

issues.message: FileReadView reuses BASH_OUTPUT_TRUNCATION_LIMIT (named for Bash) at runtime/src/tui/openclaude/tool-stubs.tsx:123. The 8KB cap is correct but the constant name implies Bash ownership. If FileRead's truncation budget should diverge from Bash's later, the shared constant will silently couple them. Consider a FILE_READ_TRUNCATION_LIMIT alias even if the value stays the same. Not a blocker — soft naming/coupling smell, consistent with how the bash row was accepted.

extractBridgeTag uses indexOf for the closing tag, which means a body containing the literal string `</read-content>` will be truncated at that point. For FileRead, this is not theoretical — a user reading a TSX/HTML file containing `</read-content>` would see partial content silently. Consider documenting this as a known limitation or escaping the body. Not a blocker — Bash and Edit have the same property and were accepted at this severity.

Positive routing asserted at runtime/src/tools/upstream-tool-result-dispatch.test.ts:86-90; negative routing for 'Read' asserted at runtime/src/tools/upstream-tool-result-dispatch.test.ts:92-96. End-to-end positive dispatch at runtime/src/tools/tool-stubs-extra-bridge.test.tsx:62-71 (expect node.type === FileReadView), end-to-end negative for 'Read' at lines 73-81 (expect node.type !== FileReadView). Cross-routing guards (FileRead + <bash-stdout> -> generic) at runtime/src/tools/upstream-tool-result-dispatch.test.ts:152-162.
behaviorEvidence.FileReadView renders the file path bolde: Implemented at runtime/src/tui/openclaude/tool-stubs.tsx:115-136 (extracts <read-file>, <read-lines>, <read-content>; headerText = lines.length > 0 ? `${file} [${lines}]` : file; renders <Text bold>{headerText}</Text> then <Text>{truncatedBody}</Text>). Asserted at runtime/src/tools/tool-stubs-extra-bridge.test.tsx:83-97 — content `<read-file>src/foo.ts</read-file>\n<read-lines>5-10</read-lines>\n<read-content>function hello() {}</read-content>`, header.props.bold true, header.props.children 'src/foo.ts [5-10]', body Text child has children 'function hello() {}'. Wire-shape-lock at runtime/src/tools/tool-stubs-extra-bridge.test.tsx:457-478 confirms the formatStructuredToolResult output is consumed by FileReadView with header 'src/foo.ts [1-3]'.
behaviorEvidence.If the content is empty, FileReadView re: Implemented at runtime/src/tui/openclaude/tool-stubs.tsx:131-133 (`{truncatedBody.length > 0 ? <Text>{truncatedBody}</Text> : <Text dimColor>(empty file)</Text>}`). Asserted at runtime/src/tools/tool-stubs-extra-bridge.test.tsx:99-108 — content `<read-file>x</read-file>\n<read-content></read-content>` produces a child with props.children === '(empty file)' and props.dimColor === true.
behaviorEvidence.message-adapter.formatStructuredToolResu: Implemented at runtime/src/tui/openclaude/message-adapter.ts:375-404 (FileRead branch: emits <read-file> when path is a string, <read-lines>{startLine}-{endLine}</read-lines> when both are numbers, then <read-content>{content}</read-content>). Envelope shape asserted at runtime/src/tui/openclaude/message-adapter.test.ts:240-255 (3-block sequence: `<read-file>src/foo.ts</read-file>`, `<read-lines>10-20</read-lines>`, `<read-content>function hello() { return 42; }</read-content>`). Round-trip consumption by FileReadView asserted at runtime/src/tools/tool-stubs-extra-bridge.test.tsx:457-478.
behaviorEvidence.Pre-seeded createBridgeTools list now co: Implemented at runtime/src/tui/openclaude/tool-stubs.tsx:471-484 (the unique Set contains 'Bash', 'Edit', 'FileRead', 'Write', 'Grep', 'Glob' and does NOT contain 'Read'). Asserted at runtime/src/tools/tool-stubs-extra-bridge.test.tsx:53-59 — `mod.createBridgeTools([])` returns a list whose `.name` set contains 'FileRead' and does NOT contain 'Read'. Asserted at runtime/src/tools/tool-stubs-extra-bridge.test.tsx:129-143 — `huge = 'x'.repeat(50_000)` is rendered, body Text child's children string contains 'chars truncated' AND has length < 10_000.

edgeCaseEvidence.FileRead with non-string startLine/endLi: Implementation guard at runtime/src/tui/openclaude/message-adapter.ts:392-397 (`if (typeof r.startLine === 'number' && typeof r.endLine === 'number')` only emits <read-lines> when both are numeric). Asserted at runtime/src/tui/openclaude/message-adapter.test.ts:257-276 — both string-typed startLine/endLine and partial-numeric (one number + one string) produce no block whose text contains '<read-lines>'.
